{"society_power": 6.5, "state_power": 8.5, "society_change": -0.5, "state_change": 1.0, "key_event": "9/11 and War on Terror expand the security state", "reasoning": "Post-9/11 surveillance, PATRIOT Act, DHS, executive war/detention powers, and the TARP intervention markedly expanded state capacity, while polarization, inequality, and deferring dissent modestly weakened broad-based society despite reactive judicial and electoral checks."}
